title: UX Acceptance Brief
description: Per-screen UX contract written early in the pipeline and consumed by every evaluation step. Travels the full pipeline as a stable reference.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this file was first created

generated_by.description: Which agent(s) produced this file (e.g. 'pipeline-intake-agent + journey-mapper')

primary_action.description: The single most important action a user must find and complete in this product

success_emotion.description: What a user should feel at the end of completing the primary action (e.g. 'delighted — feels in control')

hardest_persona.description: The persona the design must not fail — most constrained user (1–2 sentences describing constraints)

screens.description: Per-screen UX contract entries. Populated by journey-mapper and enriched by wireframe-strategist.

screen_id.description: Matches screen_id in requirements.json and user-flows.json

user_goal.description: What the user is trying to accomplish on this screen (from journey step action)

goal_alignment.description: How directly this screen serves the user's primary goal

emotion_target.description: Target emotional state for the user on this screen

emotion_risk.description: Set when journey step emotion was frustrated/confused/blocked — note the risk

primary_cta_slot_id.description: slot_id from screen-blueprints.json for the primary CTA. Null until wireframe-strategist runs.

max_cognitive_items.description: Maximum number of above-fold content priority 1+2 items. Null until wireframe-strategist runs.

required_states.description: Which states must be built for this screen

reachability_max_taps.description: Maximum number of taps to reach this screen from an entry point. Null until wireframe-strategist runs. Default 3; tertiary flows (depth > 2) use 4.

accessibility_priority.description: high = must pass WCAG AAA; standard = WCAG AA minimum

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable assertion strings derived from journey step opportunities
